text.title: Type Hint Anti-Patterns
text.summary: Optional mixing, from __future__ import annotations with Pydantic v1, Generic[T] no-op, and bare type: ignore.

antipattern.description: Mixing `Optional[X]` and `X | None` in the same file or codebase — some imports use the old form, others the new.
antipattern.reason: Mixed styles confuse `ruff --fix` idempotency, create inconsistent grep results, and make the codebase look unmaintained. Pick one style and enforce it via ruff UP007. Use `ruff check --select UP007 --fix` for a batch migration.

section.title: from __future__ import annotations with Pydantic v1

antipattern.description: Adding `from __future__ import annotations` to modules that define Pydantic v1 models.
antipattern.reason: Pydantic v1 evaluates annotations at class definition time using `get_type_hints()`. `from __future__ import annotations` defers evaluation to strings, which Pydantic v1 cannot resolve — field types become strings and validation silently breaks. Pydantic v2 handles this correctly. Verify the Pydantic version before adding future annotations.

antipattern.description: Declaring a class as `Generic[T]` but never using `T` in any method signature or field type.
antipattern.reason: A `Generic[T]` class that does not use `T` provides no type safety — it is checked as if `T = Any`. The type parameter exists only as noise. Either use `T` in at least one method signature or remove the `Generic[T]` declaration entirely.

example.note: Generic[T] declared but T never used — type safety is absent

antipattern.description: Using `# type: ignore` without an error code instead of `# type: ignore[code]`.
antipattern.reason: Bare `# type: ignore` silences all future mypy errors on that line, including unrelated ones introduced later. `warn_unused_ignores = true` in mypy config also cannot clean up stale bare ignores properly. Always scope to a specific error code.

section.title: Annotating __init__ Parameters Not Used as Fields

antipattern.description: Annotating `self` in regular methods with a type other than the implicit `Self` type, or omitting `-> None` on `__init__`.
antipattern.reason: mypy infers `self` type automatically in regular methods. Annotating `self: MyClass` is valid but redundant. `__init__` must return `None` — omitting `-> None` does not cause an error but inconsistently breaks the rule of annotating all return types.
